query: Nutrition study requires a gated inference workflow for daily calories [1850,1920,2010,1780,1960,2050,1890,1830,1975,2020]. First check normality, then run a one-sample t-test versus 2000 only if normality passes.
Investigate whether an extreme value distorts association between A=[1,2,3,4,5,50] and B=[2,4,6,8,10,12]. First detect outliers in A, then compute correlation on the cleaned data.

Web performance triage for load times [210,215,220,218,212,680,214,219,211,217]: first generate descriptive stats on the raw series, then detect outliers, then compute a 95% confidence interval using cleaned data.

Demand-model sanity check with temperature [15,18,22,25,30] and ice cream sales [200,250,320,380,450]. First compute z-scores, then correlation, then fit linear regression on the original scale and report slope.
For daily returns [-0.5,0.3,0.8,-0.2,1.2,0.5,-0.1,0.9,0.4,5.8,-4.2,0.6], run a distribution audit in order: quartiles, IQR, outlier detection, then a 95% confidence interval on the cleaned series.
Clinical pain-score assessment for [3,4,3,5,4,3,4,6,3,4]: first verify normality, second summarize descriptive stats, third run a one-sample t-test against mean 5 to test the reduction claim.
Revenue trend analysis for weekly values [42,45,43,47,50,48,52,55,53,57,60,58]: first apply a 3-week moving average, then fit linear regression on the smoothed series to estimate trend.
For paired data X=[1,2,3,4,5] and Y=[2,4,6,8,10], first calculate covariance to understand co-movement, then correlation for standardized association, then fit linear regression and report R-squared.

For returns data [0.02,-0.01,0.03,0.015,-0.005,0.025,0.01,-0.02,0.018,0.012], first compute skewness to assess asymmetry, then kurtosis for tail behavior, then run a normality test to confirm distribution shape.
For delivery times [24,18,36,22,19,42,21,28,17,35,20,30], first compute quartiles to understand spread, then IQR for variability context, then detect outliers that may indicate logistics issues.
